concepts.title: Complex numbers
concepts.blurb: Arithmetic on $\mathbb{C} = \mathbb{R}^2$ with multiplication; polar form $z = re^{i\theta}$ and the one-point compactification to the Riemann sphere $\hat{\mathbb{C}}$ via stereographic projection.

concepts.title: Riemann sphere
concepts.blurb: Stereographic projection; ∞ as a point; circles/lines unified.

concepts.title: Möbius transformations
concepts.blurb: Linear-fractional maps, cross-ratio, automorphisms of Ĉ.

concepts.title: Amplitwist
concepts.blurb: Needham's picture: a holomorphic $f$ acts on infinitesimal vectors at $z_0$ as rotation by $\arg f'(z_0)$ and scaling by $|f'(z_0)|$; the Jacobian is a similarity matrix.

concepts.title: Cauchy–Riemann equations
concepts.blurb: A complex-differentiable $f = u + iv$ must have Jacobian commuting with multiplication by $i$; equivalently $u_x = v_y$, $u_y = -v_x$, i.e. $\bar\partial f = 0$.

concepts.title: Holomorphic functions
concepts.blurb: Complex-differentiable on an open set; infinitely more rigid than real.

concepts.title: Domain coloring
concepts.blurb: Visualize $f\colon\mathbb{C}\to\mathbb{C}$ with hue $=\arg f(z)$ and lightness encoding $|f(z)|$; zeros and poles appear as rainbow winds counting multiplicity.

concepts.title: Contour integrals
concepts.blurb: Integrate $f\,dz$ along a piecewise smooth $\gamma$; the real 1-forms $u\,dx - v\,dy$ and $v\,dx + u\,dy$ are closed exactly when $f$ is holomorphic.

concepts.title: Cauchy's theorem
concepts.blurb: ∫_γ f dz = 0 on simply connected domains.

concepts.title: Cauchy integral formula
concepts.blurb: $f(a) = \tfrac{1}{2\pi i}\oint f(z)/(z-a)\,dz$: boundary values determine interior values on a closed disk, and differentiation under the integral makes holomorphic functions $C^\infty$.

concepts.title: Analyticity = holomorphy
concepts.blurb: Every holomorphic function is locally a convergent power series.

concepts.title: Liouville's theorem
concepts.blurb: Bounded entire ⇒ constant.

concepts.title: Fundamental theorem of algebra
concepts.blurb: Every non-constant polynomial over $\mathbb{C}$ has a root; Liouville's argument applied to $1/p(z)$ makes this a corollary of complex boundedness.

concepts.title: Maximum modulus principle
concepts.blurb: Non-constant holomorphic f has no interior max of |f|.

concepts.title: Schwarz lemma
concepts.blurb: For holomorphic $f\colon\mathbb{D}\to\mathbb{D}$ with $f(0)=0$: $|f(z)|\le|z|$ and $|f'(0)|\le 1$; equality forces $f(z)=e^{i\theta}z$, a rigidity extracted from the maximum modulus principle.

concepts.title: Laurent series
concepts.blurb: Expansions on annuli; principal part captures singular behavior.

concepts.title: Classification of singularities
concepts.blurb: Removable, pole of order n, essential (Casorati–Weierstrass).

concepts.title: Residue theorem
concepts.blurb: ∮ f dz = 2πi · Σ Res(f; z_k). Computes real integrals, sums series.

concepts.title: Argument principle
concepts.blurb: (1/2πi) ∮ f′/f = Z − P; winding of f(γ) counts zeros minus poles.

concepts.title: Rouché's theorem
concepts.blurb: |g| < |f| on γ ⇒ f and f+g have the same number of zeros inside.

concepts.title: Conformal maps
concepts.blurb: Angle-preserving, orientation-preserving; locally holomorphic with f′ ≠ 0.

concepts.title: Disk automorphisms
concepts.blurb: $\mathrm{Aut}(\mathbb{D})$ consists of the Blaschke factors $e^{i\theta}(z-a)/(1-\bar a z)$; this is the isometry group $\mathrm{PSU}(1,1)$ of the hyperbolic Poincaré metric on the disk.

concepts.title: Normal families (Montel)
concepts.blurb: Montel: a locally bounded family of holomorphic functions is normal (every sequence has a locally uniformly convergent subsequence); compactness that powers the Riemann mapping theorem.

concepts.title: Riemann mapping theorem
concepts.blurb: Any simply connected proper open U ⊂ C is conformally equivalent to D.

concepts.title: Harmonic functions
concepts.blurb: If $f = u + iv$ is holomorphic then $u$ and $v$ satisfy Laplace's equation $\Delta u = 0$; on simply connected domains every harmonic $u$ has a harmonic conjugate recovering a holomorphic lift.

concepts.title: Analytic continuation
concepts.blurb: The identity theorem lets a holomorphic germ extend uniquely along paths; loops around branch points produce monodromy, and $\log z$ or $\sqrt z$ live naturally on a Riemann surface rather than $\mathbb{C}\setminus\{0\}$.
